title: Non-Local SEO Engagement Plan
content_md:
# Non-Local SEO Engagement Plan

- Code: f630a6f9
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=f630a6f9bafc458ca0345583dea24531
- Video: https://vimeo.com/1094175058?share=copy
- Duration: 5m 16s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So this is a question that I get all the time. How do we manage ongoing SEO engagements with existing clients?
So let me break it down. This is gonna be pretty quick. There's only a handful of slides
What we do is pretty simple for ongoing SEO engagements
So the first month of a brand new client
We're gonna do a content audit to find the low-hanging fruit that we can hopefully rank fairly quickly
Then in month two we do it again and we basically just keep doing that except we don't redo the content audit
We just choose a different target the content audit we did on month one
Hopefully we'll have a handful of targets and depending on the scope of work the size of the engagement
We might not be able to get through all of those in the first month. So we just in second month choosing another one third month
choose another one and
Every three four five six months. We repeat the content audit to find new targets
And what we'll see is as the site ranks higher does better
We'll see rank improvements across the entire domain. So repeating the content audit finds more opportunities and identifies if the work that we did
the prior months has successfully pushed things onto the first page and
Always keeps us with plenty more work to do for that given client
that's valuable and driving results and that is basically what we do for SEO very simple just producing content or
Producing supporting content links to that supporting content
We do a content audit every three to six months
To find new targets as we keep publishing content that Google lights that content makes the site more and more authoritative
More and more comprehensive remember never publish a piece of content without sourcing an external link to it
The external link shows Google that content is good
If you skip that step Google won't know the content is any good and it won't have any effect at all
You need to publish external links to the content that you're writing all the time and yeah, no fancy tricks
Just publish good supporting content using the chat GPT methods
Get your internal linking correct and then get your external linking move on to the next target
Okay, so path forward non-local SEO just a very broad overview. Here's what we do on a regular basis, right?
We're going to do a topical relevance map sometimes you do keyword research
Sometimes we don't do keyword research, but we always do a topical relevance map topical relevance is incredibly incredibly important in Google's modern algorithm
The reason I say that I'm showing you this example cakes by MK comm
They are a masterful case study in generating topical relevance for cakes
You can see a very very low domain rating domain rating of 16. That's quite low and they're getting
200,000 traffic from 41,000 keywords and if we look at their keywords, we can see they're all about cake
Right, so they did a topical relevance map for cake and then they basically wrote every single article that they could about cakes a
Quick way. I love this site colon search. Let me pull this down so you can see what I'm doing
and
We used to show right here now. I got a kind of click
And there are two hundred and twelve results, so they wrote two hundred and twelve articles about cakes
domain rating of
16 or something 16 and
200,000 visitors a month you can see how much it's exploded. So this is why we focus on topical relevance
So we want to Google to see that our client's website our client is the leader in this space
They have a lot of expertise a lot of authority on
Whatever the topic is so we're going to create this topical relevance map
There's lessons on how to do that. You're going to get hundreds of content ideas. You're going to generate all of the content
Again using the chat GPT
Content generation section here. You're going to point links every article you publish should have a link
Otherwise Google doesn't know it's any good and then repeat you can generate more
Topical relevance ideas with similar prompts if you've written all the hundreds of articles you can add
Content that is sort of in the same space but set up a different niche silo
But essentially for non-local SEO, it's really just about
Generating a bunch of topically relevant content as I've said many times before I'll repeat it here
Do not take AI content and directly copy paste it to your website
We've seen that generally will not work very well a human editor is very important to do this and also keep in mind
Let me pull it up real quick
When we're looking at this if I look at the organic keywords and I can come and take a look at the organic keyword data
One of the things that we want to focus on is
This SERP overview down at the bottom
And again like if we're doing topical relevance, we just want the content regardless
But we might look at the domain ratings and I mean a lot of these are quite high
Cakes by MK is the only one that's low again
Like just so much content so much about cakes. They've really established topical relevance with Google's algorithm
So that's what we want to do
